evals.name: python-api-code-smells-and-patterns
evals.expected_output: A prioritized tech debt report identifying bare except, mutable default arguments, blocking calls in async, swallowed exceptions, global mutable state, deep nesting, magic numbers, and dead code. Report printed in severity-grouped table format with a summary and hotspots section. GitHub issues created.
expectations: Dispatches 3 parallel agents (Dependency/Environment, Structural/Complexity, Code Pattern scanners)
Identifies the bare except on the get_user function as Critical severity
Identifies mutable default argument def get_user(user_id, default_roles=[]) as High severity code-smell
Identifies mutable default argument def validate_email(email, allowed_domains=[]) as High severity code-smell
Identifies blocking time.sleep and requests calls inside async def send_notification as High severity async findings
Identifies swallowed exception (except Exception as e: pass) in send_notification as High severity error-handling
Identifies global mutable state (_user_cache, _rate_limits dicts) as Medium severity code-smell
Identifies magic number 8473 in REQUEST_TIMEOUT as Medium severity code-smell
Identifies deep nesting in generate_report function (5+ levels) as Medium severity
Identifies commented-out legacy_auth_handler as Low severity dead code
Report output follows the specified format: severity-grouped tables with File, Line, Finding, Category columns
Report includes a Summary table broken down by category and severity
Report includes a Hotspots section showing files with 3+ findings
Creates GitHub sub-issues per category and a parent issue linking them
anti_expectations: Does not modify any source files in the scanned scope
Does not produce false positives on correct code patterns like export_csv or validate_email logic
Does not skip the GitHub issue creation step
Does not omit code snippets from GitHub issue bodies

evals.name: business-logic-leakage-and-architecture
evals.prompt: Scan skills/tech-debt-finder/evals/files/ for tech debt — focus on the handler and model files: eval2_handlers.py and eval2_models.py
evals.expected_output: Report identifying business logic leakage in handlers (ORM calls, validation, payment processing, email sending directly in handler layer), global mutable state (ACTIVE_SESSIONS), and Any overuse. Severity-grouped tables and GitHub issues created.
expectations: Identifies business logic leakage: ORM calls (Order.create, User.query.get, order.save) directly in OrderHandler as High severity architecture finding
Identifies business logic leakage: payment processing (charge_card) called directly in create_order handler
Identifies business logic leakage: email sending (send_email) called directly in create_order handler
Identifies business logic leakage: validation logic (email/phone validation) in UserHandler.update_profile
Identifies global mutable state ACTIVE_SESSIONS dict as Medium severity code-smell
Identifies generic catch (except Exception as e) without re-raise in create_order as Medium or High severity error-handling
Deduplicates findings if same file:line appears in multiple categories, keeping highest severity
Sorts findings within each severity group by file path then line number
Each finding includes category, severity, file, and line fields
Every finding in GitHub issues includes an embedded code snippet with line numbers
Creates run-specific label tech-debt:{YYYY-MM-DD} for issue grouping
anti_expectations: Does not modify any source files
Does not flag the model file's dataclass patterns or query class stubs as debt
Does not skip creating the parent GitHub issue that links all sub-issues

evals.name: async-blocking-and-dependency-scan
evals.prompt: Run a full tech debt audit on skills/tech-debt-finder/evals/files/ — I want to see the async worker issues and dependency problems
evals.expected_output: Report covering eval3_async_worker.py async violations (blocking requests/time.sleep in async defs, bare except, deep nesting, global mutable state, dead code) and eval3_pyproject.toml dependency issues (no upper bounds on versions). All three scanners produce findings. GitHub issues created with sub-issues per category.
expectations: Agent A (Dependency scanner) identifies pinning gaps — dependencies with no upper bound (e.g., requests>=2.28 with no <3)
Agent B (Structural scanner) identifies deep nesting in generate_async_report (6 levels of if-statements) as Medium severity
Agent C (Code Pattern scanner) identifies bare except in process_task_queue as Critical severity
Identifies blocking requests.post/get calls inside async functions (send_bulk_emails, generate_async_report, sync_external_data) as High severity async findings
Identifies blocking time.sleep calls inside async functions as High severity async findings
Identifies global mutable state (TASK_QUEUE, RESULTS) as Medium or High severity code-smell
Identifies commented-out old_worker_loop as Low severity dead code
Identifies swallowed exception in process_task_queue bare except (RESULTS assignment with no logging/re-raise) as Critical or High
Report includes file count of scanned files in the header
Report prints findings in severity order: Critical, High, Medium, Low
Creates GitHub labels 'tech-debt' and run-specific 'tech-debt:{YYYY-MM-DD}' before creating issues
Parent GitHub issue contains a task list referencing all sub-issue numbers
anti_expectations: Does not modify any source files in the scanned directory
Does not skip any of the 3 parallel scanner agents
Does not create issues without code snippets embedded in the body
Does not report findings from excluded directories (node_modules, .venv, __pycache__)
